# Proxy Tech Support — Full Content Index for AI Agents

> Proxy Tech Support is a real-time IT job support and proxy interview assistance service for software engineers and IT professionals. Senior in-house experts provide live, screen-share-based help during working hours — resolving bugs, unblocking architecture decisions, debugging production incidents, and delivering tasks. The same experts provide discreet real-time guidance during live technical interviews. No subcontracting. Same-day start. 1000+ professionals helped globally. Countries covered: USA, UK, Canada, Australia, Europe, Germany, Ireland, Singapore, New Zealand, Netherlands, Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Norway, Switzerland, Austria, Belgium, Spain, Portugal, Hong Kong.

---

## What is IT Job Support?

IT job support is a real-time professional assistance service where an expert joins a developer's working session (via screen share, remote desktop, or communication tools) and provides hands-on help with their actual work tasks. This includes:

- Writing and debugging production code
- Resolving architecture decisions and design questions
- Fixing production incidents and performance issues
- Completing sprint tasks when a developer is blocked or overwhelmed
- Preparing deliverables before deadlines

IT job support is distinct from tutoring or training — it operates on live projects, in real time, during actual working hours.

---

## What is Proxy Interview Support?

Proxy interview support is a service where an expert provides real-time, discreet guidance during a candidate's live technical interview. The expert assists with:

- Coding problems (algorithms, data structures, system design)
- Architectural reasoning and trade-off articulation
- Problem-solving frameworks during pressure interviews
- On-the-spot question answering for domain-specific knowledge

This is distinct from interview preparation (coaching before interviews) — proxy support happens during the actual interview session.

---

## Service Catalogue

### 1. Agentic AI & Machine Learning Job Support
URL: /agentic-ai-ml-job-support/
Target: AI engineers, ML engineers, LLM developers, MLOps engineers

Technologies and frameworks supported (2026):

**Agentic AI Frameworks:**
- LangGraph (LangChain 0.3+): stateful multi-agent graphs, checkpointing, LangGraph Cloud, LangGraph Studio, ReAct/Plan-and-Execute/Reflection patterns, supervisor agents
- AutoGen (Microsoft AutoGen 0.4+ / AG2): AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent, GroupChat, AutoGen Studio, Docker execution sandboxes, AutoGen Core async event-driven agents
- CrewAI (0.80+): Agents, Tasks, Processes (sequential/hierarchical/parallel), Flows, entity memory, long-term memory
- OpenAI Agents SDK (v1.x): handoffs, guardrails, Responses API, swarm patterns, streaming, tracing
- Pydantic AI: type-safe agent construction
- Smolagents (Hugging Face): code agents, Python execution, multi-step reasoning
- Semantic Kernel (Microsoft 1.x): plugins, planners, memory stores
- LlamaIndex Workflows: event-driven agentic pipelines, Steps, Context

**RAG Architectures (2026):**
- HyDE (Hypothetical Document Embeddings)
- GraphRAG (Microsoft): knowledge graph extraction, community summarization
- Self-RAG: reflection tokens, factuality grading
- Corrective RAG (CRAG): quality evaluation, web fallback
- Adaptive RAG: query classification, routing
- Multi-Query Retrieval, Parent Document Retrieval
- Contextual Compression, Contextual Retrieval (Anthropic)
- LightRAG: graph+vector hybrid retrieval
- FLARE: forward-looking active retrieval

**Embeddings:**
- OpenAI text-embedding-3-large (3072-dim), text-embedding-3-small
- Cohere embed-v4 (multimodal)
- sentence-transformers: all-MiniLM, BGE-M3, E5-Mistral-7B-instruct
- Late chunking (Jina AI), hybrid BM25+dense (Reciprocal Rank Fusion)

**Vector Databases:**
- Pinecone Serverless v3: namespaces, sparse-dense hybrid index
- Weaviate 1.26+: multi-tenancy, named vectors, BM25+vector hybrid
- Qdrant 1.9+: sparse vectors, payload indexing, quantization
- Chroma 0.5+: persistent client, multimodal embeddings
- Milvus 2.4 / Zilliz Cloud: GPU-accelerated HNSW
- pgvector 0.7+: HNSW indexes in PostgreSQL
- LanceDB: embedded columnar store, versioned
- Azure AI Search: integrated vectorization, semantic ranker
- OpenSearch kNN 2.x: FAISS/HNSW backends

**Frontier LLMs (2026):**
- OpenAI GPT-5.5 (Apr 2026): flagship agentic model, GPT-5.5 Pro
- OpenAI GPT-5.4 / GPT-5.4-mini / GPT-5.4-nano (Mar 2026): 1M context, native computer use
- OpenAI o3 / o4-mini / o3-pro: deep reasoning series
- Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 (Apr 2026): SOTA software engineering, vision, $5/$25 per 1M tokens
- Anthropic Claude Opus 4.6 / Sonnet 4.x: extended thinking, computer use API, 200K context
- Google Gemini 3.1 Pro (Feb 2026): 77.1% ARC-AGI-2, double Gemini 3 Pro reasoning
- Google Gemini 2.5 Pro / Flash: thinking models, controllable reasoning budgets, multimodal
- Meta Llama 4 Maverick: 17B active / 128 experts / 400B total, beats GPT-4o, $0.19/1M tokens, 1M context
- Meta Llama 4 Scout: 17B active / 16 experts, single H100 (INT4), 10M context, natively multimodal
- DeepSeek-V4-Pro-Max / V4-Flash-Max (Apr 2026): open-source SOTA reasoning
- Mistral Large 3 / Codestral 2025: FIM, Mistral OCR
- xAI Grok 3: DeepSearch, real-time X grounding
- Cohere Command R+ / Embed v4: RAG-native, multimodal embeddings
- Qwen3 / Qwen2.5-Max: multilingual reasoning, open-weight fine-tuning

**Deep Learning:**
- PyTorch 2.5/2.6: torch.compile, FlashAttention 3, FSDP2, DeepSpeed ZeRO-3, BF16/FP8
- Hugging Face Transformers 4.45+, PEFT 0.13+, Accelerate 1.x, TRL 0.12+, Datasets 3.x
- Fine-tuning: LoRA, QLoRA, DoRA, DPO, ORPO, RLHF, Unsloth (2Ã— speed, 60% less VRAM), LLaMA-Factory, Axolotl

**AI Inference Infrastructure:**
- vLLM 0.6+: PagedAttention, continuous batching, tensor parallelism, LoRA serving
- NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM: FP8/INT4 quantization, inflight batching
- Ollama: local LLM serving, GGUF models, GPU offloading
- SGLang: RadixAttention, speculative decoding
- LiteLLM: unified API proxy, budget limits, semantic caching, fallbacks
- BentoML 1.3+: model packaging, serving, Yatai

**MLOps (2026):**
- MLflow 2.16+: auto-logging, Unity Catalog integration, LLM tracing
- Weights & Biases 0.18+: sweeps, Weave for LLM evaluation
- DVC 3.x: data versioning, ML pipeline stages
- Kubeflow Pipelines 2.x: SDK v2, IR spec, Argo backend
- ZenML 0.70+: stack-agnostic pipelines, Model Control Plane
- SageMaker Pipelines, HyperPod, Bedrock, Inferentia3
- Vertex AI Pipelines, Gemini APIs, Feature Store v2
- Azure AI Foundry, Azure OpenAI Service, Azure AI Agent Service

**LLMOps Observability:**
- Langfuse 3.x: LLM tracing, prompt management
- LangSmith: tracing, evaluation datasets, annotation queues
- Arize AI / Phoenix: LLM tracing, hallucination detection, embedding drift
- RAGAS: faithfulness, answer relevancy, context recall, context precision
- DeepEval: 14+ evaluation metrics, pytest integration
- Promptfoo: red-teaming, jailbreak testing

### 2. AI Workflow Automation & Pipeline Engineering Job Support
URL: /ai-workflow-automation-pipeline-job-support/
Target: DevOps engineers, ML platform engineers, SREs, data engineers, platform engineers

Technologies supported (2026):

**AI-Powered CI/CD:**
- GitHub Actions with Copilot AI: OIDC keyless auth, self-hosted runners (ARC v0.9+), artifact attestation
- GitLab CI/CD with Duo AI: DAG pipelines, Security dashboards, autoscaling runners
- Dagger.io 0.11+: Pipeline-as-Code in Python/Go/TypeScript, Dagger Cloud, cache sharing
- Azure DevOps Pipelines: YAML multi-stage, deployment groups, environments
- Tekton Pipelines: Kubernetes-native, Tasks, PipelineRuns, Triggers
- Buildkite, CircleCI

**Workflow Orchestration:**
- Airflow 2.10+: TaskFlow API, dynamic task mapping, KubernetesPodOperator, Astronomer Astro, dataset-triggered DAGs
- Prefect 3.x: flows, tasks, workers, work pools, deployments, Prefect Cloud, automations
- Dagster 1.x: software-defined assets, asset checks, partitioned assets, branch deployments, Dagster+
- Temporal.io: durable workflow execution, Temporal Cloud, Temporal Nexus, human-in-the-loop approvals
- n8n 1.x: AI Agent nodes, LangChain integration, 400+ integrations, self-hosted
- AWS Step Functions + Bedrock, Azure Logic Apps + Azure AI, Google Cloud Workflows + Vertex AI
- Windmill.dev

**MLOps Pipeline Engineering:**
- Kubeflow Pipelines 2.x: SDK v2, IR spec, KFP components, Katib, KServe
- ZenML 0.70+: stack components, artifact stores, Model Control Plane
- SageMaker Pipelines: Processing/Training/Evaluation/Condition steps, model registry, Clarify
- Vertex AI Pipelines: KFP backend, Vizier, feature store integration
- Metaflow, Prefect for ML, DVC

**GitOps & Deployment:**
- ArgoCD 2.12+: ApplicationSets, Image Updater, Argo Rollouts (Blue/Green, Canary), notifications
- Flux CD 2.x: OCI artifacts, Kustomize/Helm controllers, Flagger progressive delivery
- Argo Workflows: container-native DAG CI/CD

**Infrastructure as Code:**
- Terraform 1.9+: ephemeral resources, provider functions, import blocks
- OpenTofu 1.8+: open-source Terraform fork, provider-defined functions
- Pulumi: Automation API, ESC, Insights, Copilot AI
- Ansible 10.x / Automation Platform 2.5: Event-Driven Ansible (EDA), Lightspeed AI
- AWS CDK v2: L2/L3 constructs, CDK Pipelines, Nag security checks
- Azure Bicep 0.29+, Crossplane

**AIOps & Observability:**
- Datadog: Bits AI assistant, Watchdog anomaly detection, LLM Observability, CI Visibility, Workflows
- Dynatrace: Davis AI, Davis CoPilot, Grail lakehouse (DQL), AutomationEngine, OpenPipeline
- Grafana 11+: Beyla eBPF auto-instrumentation, Loki 3.x, Tempo 2.x, k6 AI, OnCall
- New Relic AI, PagerDuty AIOps, Elastic Observability 8.x
- OpenTelemetry 1.x: OTLP, Collector 0.100+

**Kubernetes for AI Workloads:**
- Kubernetes 1.32: sidecar containers GA, structured auth, in-place pod resize
- KEDA 2.15+: Kafka/SQS/Prometheus/custom metric scalers
- Karpenter 1.x: GPU-aware scheduling, spot interruption handling
- NVIDIA GPU Operator 24.x: MIG partitioning, driver installation
- KubeAI: Kubernetes-native LLM serving, Ollama/vLLM backends
- Helm 3.16+, Helmfile, Istio 1.23+ ambient mode, Linkerd 2.15+, Kyverno 1.12+

**Platform Engineering:**
- Backstage 1.30+: AI-powered scaffolder, software catalog, TechDocs, AI search plugin
- Port.io: self-service developer portal, scorecards, AI agents
- Humanitec Platform Orchestrator, Kratix, Cortex

**LLMOps Deployment:**
- LangServe / FastAPI: deploying LangChain chains as REST endpoints
- Ray Serve 2.x: scalable model serving, batching, autoscaling
- Triton Inference Server 24.x: multi-framework serving, FP8/INT4
- KServe 0.14+: Kubernetes-native model serving, InferenceService
- LiteLLM Proxy: centralized routing, budget limits, fallbacks
- Langfuse 3.x, PromptLayer, Agenta

### 3. Data Science Job Support
URL: /data-science-job-support/
Target: Data scientists, ML engineers, data analysts, business intelligence developers

Technologies supported (2026):

**Python Data Stack:**
- pandas 2.2+: Copy-on-Write, PyArrow backend, method chaining
- Polars 1.x: lazy evaluation, Rust-based performance, expression API
- NumPy 2.0+: new dtype system, DLPack protocol
- DuckDB 1.x: in-process OLAP SQL, Arrow integration
- DataFusion: SQL analytics for large local datasets

**Visualization:**
- Matplotlib 3.9+, Seaborn 0.13+ (objects API), Plotly 5.x, Altair 5.x, Bokeh 3.x
- Streamlit 1.35+, Gradio 4.x

**Machine Learning:**
- scikit-learn 1.5+: TunedThresholdClassifierCV, set_output transform='pandas'
- XGBoost 2.1+: multi-output trees, GPU training device='cuda'
- LightGBM 4.x: GOSS/EFB, categorical handling
- CatBoost 1.2+: ordered boosting, GPU
- AutoML: FLAML, AutoGluon 1.x, H2O AutoML

**Explainability:**
- SHAP 0.46+: TreeExplainer, GPU acceleration
- Alibi 0.9+: counterfactuals, anchors, Integrated Gradients
- InterpretML: Explainable Boosting Machine (EBM)

**Statistics & Causal Inference:**
- SciPy 1.14+, Statsmodels 0.14+, PyMC 5.x, ArviZ
- DoWhy 0.11+: causal graphs, identification, estimation
- EconML: CATE, DML, causal forests, IV regression
- CausalML: uplift modeling, meta-learners

**Big Data:**
- PySpark 3.5+: Connect mode, MLlib, Structured Streaming
- Databricks Runtime 15.x: Delta Lake 4.x, Unity Catalog, Mosaic AI
- Delta Live Tables, Databricks AutoML, Feature Engineering
- Dask 2024.x, Ray 2.x, Apache Flink (PyFlink)

**Time Series:**
- Prophet (Meta), statsforecast (Nixtla), neuralforecast, Darts 0.30+, sktime 0.30+
- TimeGPT (Nixtla): foundation model for zero-shot forecasting

**NLP Data Science:**
- spaCy 3.7+, NLTK 3.9+, Gensim 4.x, BERTopic
- sentence-transformers 3.x, Hugging Face BERT/RoBERTa fine-tuning

**Data Engineering for Data Scientists:**
- dbt 1.8+: SQL models, semantic layer, dbt Mesh
- Airflow 2.9+, Prefect 3.x, Great Expectations
- Delta Lake / Iceberg / Hudi, SQLMesh 0.120+

**BI & Reporting:**
- Tableau 2024.x, Power BI 2025, Apache Superset 4.x, Evidence 35+

### 4. SRE & Reliability Engineering Job Support USA
URL: /sre-job-support-usa/
Target: Site Reliability Engineers, platform engineers, DevOps engineers with SRE responsibilities, on-call engineers

Focus: Day-to-day SRE work support — not interview coaching (see SRE Proxy Interview Support for that). Expert help with real production reliability engineering tasks during US working hours.

Technologies and areas supported:

**SLO/SLI/Error Budget Implementation:**
- SLI selection for specific service types (latency percentile selection, error rate definition, availability calculation)
- SLO configuration in Prometheus, Datadog, or custom observability stacks
- Multi-window multi-burn-rate alerting rules (Google SRE methodology)
- Error budget dashboards in Grafana
- SLO reporting automation for engineering and product leadership

**Incident Response & On-Call Support:**
- Real-time root cause analysis during live production incidents
- Incident command facilitation (IC, comms lead, operations lead structure)
- PagerDuty and OpsGenie escalation policy design
- On-call rotation structure and alert deduplication
- Blameless postmortem writing and facilitation
- Runbook design and automation

**Observability Stack:**
- Prometheus: recording rules, alerting rules, federation, remote write
- Grafana 11+: dashboard design, alerting, OnCall, Loki integration
- OpenTelemetry: instrumentation strategy, trace sampling policy, OTLP collector configuration
- Distributed tracing: Jaeger, Tempo (Grafana), Zipkin backend selection and configuration
- Structured logging pipelines
- Datadog, Dynatrace, New Relic — SRE-specific configuration

**Chaos Engineering:**
- Chaos experiment design (steady-state hypothesis, blast radius scoping)
- Gremlin: dependency latency injection, resource exhaustion, network partition
- LitmusChaos: Kubernetes chaos experiments, ChaosEngine CRDs
- Chaos Monkey: Netflix Simian Army approach
- GameDay facilitation design
- Chaos in production vs staging trade-offs

**Toil Automation:**
- Toil identification and measurement frameworks
- Manual runbook replacement with automation (bots, scripts, self-healing)
- Alert correlation and deduplication scripts
- Deployment validation automation

**Capacity Planning:**
- Demand forecasting from historical traffic data
- Load testing: k6, Locust, Gatling — test design and result interpretation
- Headroom policy decisions and scaling recommendations
- Traffic management under peak load

**Multi-Region Reliability:**
- Active-active vs active-passive trade-off analysis
- DNS failover configuration and testing
- RPO/RTO derivation and validation
- Circuit breaker, bulkhead, rate limiting architecture

---

## Expert Blog Articles — SRE & Observability

### Golden Signals Dashboard for SRE — Datadog Article
URL: /blog/golden-signals-dashboard-sre-datadog-success-rate-availability/
Type: Expert technical blog article
Published: 2026-05-19
Target audience: SRE engineers, DevOps engineers, platform engineers, Datadog users, observability engineers, SRE and DevOps interview candidates, production support engineers

Primary SEO topics: Golden Signals Dashboard, SRE Golden Signals Dashboard, Datadog Golden Signals Dashboard, Success Rate Dashboard, Availability Dashboard, SRE Dashboard, Datadog SLO Dashboard, Error Budget Dashboard, SLO Burn Rate Dashboard, Datadog troubleshooting widgets, SRE job support, Datadog job support, SRE proxy interview support, DevOps SRE interview support, production support dashboard, observability dashboard, incident troubleshooting dashboard

**Article Summary:**
A complete production-grade guide to building a Golden Signals dashboard in Datadog for SRE teams. Sourced from real SRE dashboard requirements documents. Covers the full 9-section layout, all 29 required widgets, Datadog template variables, formula patterns, troubleshooting investigation flows, SLO/error budget mechanics, and how to explain this dashboard in SRE interviews.

**Opening premise:** A Golden Signals dashboard should answer one question fast — is the service reliable right now, and if not, where should we investigate first? Every design decision must serve that goal.

**Four Golden Signals (practical SRE interpretation):**
- Latency: P50/P95/P99 — not average; P50 for typical users, P95 for 1-in-20 users, P99 for SLA commitments; gap between P50 and P99 reveals tail latency issues
- Traffic: request volume per second/minute; required to contextualize all other signals (2% error rate on 10 RPS vs 50,000 RPS is completely different severity)
- Errors: rate of failed requests broken down by HTTP status code (4xx vs 5xx), endpoint, error type/exception class, region, version
- Saturation: CPU, memory, pod restarts, OOM kills, replica availability (desired vs running vs available), connection pool (active/idle/wait/timeout), queue depth, consumer lag

**Success Rate vs Availability (key distinction):**
- Success Rate = Successful Requests / Total Requests Ã— 100 (request-level reliability; degrades when service receives traffic and returns errors)
- Availability = Successful Health Checks / Total Health Checks Ã— 100 (reachability/uptime reliability; degrades when service is unreachable or failing health checks)
- Both metrics are required; they explain different failure modes
- Data sources: Success Rate from APM traces and application metrics; Availability from Synthetic monitors, health check endpoints, uptime monitors, SLO availability rollup

**Dashboard Layout (9 rows):**
1. Current Service Health: Current Success Rate, Current Availability, Current Error Rate, P95 Latency, Request Volume, SLO/Error Budget (query value widgets with color thresholds)
2. Progression Over Time: Success Rate Over Time (with 99.9% target line), Availability Over Time, SLO Burn Rate, Error Budget Remaining (timeseries)
3. Failure Analysis: Error Rate Over Time (grouped by status code/endpoint/region/version), HTTP Status Code Breakdown, Top Failing Endpoints, Error Type Breakdown
4. Latency: P50/P95/P99 Latency, Slowest Endpoints, Dependency Latency, Latency by Region
5. Traffic: Request Rate Over Time, Traffic by Endpoint, Traffic by Region, Traffic vs Error Rate
6. Saturation: CPU and Memory, Pod Restarts/OOM, Replica Availability, Connection Pool Usage
7. Dependencies: Database Health, Cache Health, Queue Health, External API Health
8. Logs and Traces: Recent Error Logs (with trace_id), Top Error Messages, Trace Samples (failed/slow spans), Logs by Endpoint
9. Deployment Correlation: Deployment Events overlay, Success Rate by Version, Error Rate by Version, Latency by Version

**Datadog Template Variables:**
$env (env tag), $service (service tag), $region (region tag), $availability_zone (availability-zone tag), $cluster (kube_cluster_name), $namespace (kube_namespace), $pod (pod_name), $host (host), $version (version tag)

**Widget Thresholds:**
- Current Success Rate: green â‰¥99.9%, yellow 99.5–99.9%, red <99.5%
- Current Availability: green â‰¥99.9%, yellow 99.5–99.9%, red <99.5%
- Current Error Rate: green <0.1%, yellow 0.1–0.5%, red >0.5%
- P95 Latency: green <500ms, yellow 500ms–1000ms, red >1000ms

**SLO Burn Rate mechanics:**
- Burn rate 1 = consuming budget at exactly the sustainable rate
- Burn rate 14.4 over 1 hour = critical alert (exhausting 30-day budget in ~2 days)
- Burn rate 6 over 6 hours = slow burn alert
- Reference lines at 1, 6, and 14.4 on the burn rate timeseries widget

**Troubleshooting Flows (5 symptom patterns):**
1. Success Rate drops â†’ Error Rate Over Time â†’ HTTP Status Code Breakdown â†’ Top Failing Endpoints â†’ Error Type Breakdown â†’ Recent Deployments â†’ Dependency Health â†’ Logs/Traces â†’ Saturation
2. Availability drops â†’ Availability Monitor â†’ Region/AZ Availability â†’ Health Check Failures â†’ Pod Restarts â†’ Replica Availability â†’ Load Balancer Health â†’ Dependency Outage â†’ Recent Deployments
3. Latency increases â†’ P95/P99 Latency â†’ Slowest Endpoints â†’ Dependency Latency â†’ Database Latency â†’ Queue Lag â†’ CPU/Memory â†’ Traffic Spike â†’ Recent Deployment
4. Traffic spike â†’ Request Rate â†’ Traffic by Endpoint â†’ Traffic by Region â†’ Error Rate â†’ Saturation â†’ Autoscaling/Replicas â†’ Connection Pool
5. Errors after deployment â†’ Deployment Events overlay â†’ Error Rate by Version â†’ Success Rate by Version â†’ Latency by Version â†’ Logs/Traces filtered by version â†’ Rollback decision

**Datadog Query Patterns:**
- Success Rate: (sum of success metric / sum of total request metric) Ã— 100, using .as_count() aggregation
- Error Rate: (sum of error metric / sum of total request metric) Ã— 100
- Availability: (sum of healthy checks / sum of total checks) Ã— 100
- SLO Burn Rate: error_rate_in_window / allowed_error_rate_for_SLO
- P95 Latency by endpoint: p95 of latency metric grouped by resource_name
- Pod restarts: sum of kubernetes.containers.restarts by pod_name as count

**Interview angle:** How SRE candidates should describe this dashboard in interviews — frame around production decisions, not feature lists. Explain burn rate alerting threshold rationale (14.4x = 2-day budget exhaustion). Differentiate Availability vs Success Rate as two distinct failure modes.

**Internal links in article:**
- /sre-job-support-usa/ (real-time SRE job support)
- /sre-proxy-interview-support/ (proxy interview support for SRE roles)
- /devops-job-support-usa/ (DevOps job support)
- /devops-proxy-interview-support/ (DevOps proxy interview support)
- /cloud-job-support-usa/ (cloud and DevOps job support)
- /job-support-usa/ (IT job support USA)
- /proxy-interview-usa/, /proxy-interview-canada/, /proxy-interview-uk/, /proxy-interview-australia/
- /technologies/, /interview-questions/, /blog/

**FAQs included (with structured data):**
1. What is a Golden Signals Dashboard in SRE?
2. What are the four golden signals of monitoring?
3. How do you calculate Success Rate in Datadog?
4. What is the difference between Success Rate and Availability?
5. Which Datadog widgets are needed for SRE troubleshooting?
6. How do SLO, error budget, and burn rate help SRE teams?
7. How should an SRE explain Golden Signals in an interview?
8. Can Proxy Tech Support help with SRE Datadog job support?
9. Can Proxy Tech Support help with SRE or DevOps interview preparation?

## Proxy Interview Support — Technology-Specific Pages

### Proxy Interview Support — By Technology

**AI/ML Proxy Interview Support**
URL: /ai-ml-proxy-interview-support/
Target: AI engineers, ML engineers interviewing at tech companies, AI-native startups, and enterprises
Focus: Live guidance during actual AI/ML technical interviews — NOT MLOps infrastructure (see MLOps proxy page)
Topics: Deep learning architectures, PyTorch model development, LLM fine-tuning (LoRA/QLoRA/DPO), RAG pipeline design, recommendation systems, NLP, computer vision, ML system design, model evaluation, feature engineering, production AI deployment

**MLOps Proxy Interview Support**
URL: /mlops-proxy-interview-support/
Target: MLOps engineers, ML platform engineers interviewing for infrastructure-focused ML roles
Focus: ML infrastructure and pipeline interviews — NOT model development (see AI/ML proxy page)
Topics: ML pipeline orchestration (Kubeflow, Airflow, ZenML), MLflow experiment tracking, feature stores, model registry, CI/CD for ML, model drift detection, LLMOps observability, SageMaker, Vertex AI, Azure ML

**DevOps Proxy Interview Support**
URL: /devops-proxy-interview-support/
Target: DevOps engineers, platform engineers, infrastructure engineers
Focus: Live guidance during DevOps technical interviews — Kubernetes architecture, GitOps, CI/CD design, Terraform IaC, cloud infrastructure at scale
Topics: Kubernetes cluster design, ArgoCD GitOps, GitHub Actions CI/CD design, Terraform modules, cloud architecture, container security, observability stack design

**Java Proxy Interview Support**
URL: /java-proxy-interview-support/
Target: Java engineers, Spring Boot developers interviewing for enterprise Java roles
Topics: Spring Boot microservices, Kafka consumer/producer design, Hibernate/JPA, Spring Security, JVM performance tuning, Java concurrency, distributed systems design with Java

**SRE Proxy Interview Support**
URL: /sre-proxy-interview-support/
Target: SRE candidates interviewing at Google, Netflix, Stripe, Cloudflare, financial services, and tech companies
Focus: SRE interviews assess production reliability philosophy — NOT operational tooling (DevOps interviews do that)
Topics: SLO/SLI design rationale, error budget policy philosophy, multi-window burn-rate alerting design, blameless postmortem facilitation, chaos engineering experiment methodology, capacity planning frameworks, incident command structure, production reliability architecture

### Proxy Interview Support — By Location

**Proxy Interview Australia**
URL: /proxy-interview-australia/
Target: IT professionals interviewing at Australian tech companies and banks
Coverage: Commonwealth Bank, ANZ, NAB, Westpac, Atlassian, Canva, REA Group, Seek, Telstra, Optus, WiseTech Global, Services Australia
Time zones: AEST/AEDT aligned

**Proxy Interview Europe**
URL: /proxy-interview-europe/
Target: IT professionals interviewing at European tech companies, banks, and enterprises
Coverage: Booking.com, ASML, ING, Adyen, Philips, SAP, Siemens, Deutsche Bank, Spotify, Klarna, EU Blue Card holders
Time zones: CET/GMT aligned

**Proxy Interview Germany**
URL: /proxy-interview-germany/
Target: IT professionals interviewing at German tech companies, banks, and enterprises — distinct from the broader /proxy-interview-europe/ page
Coverage focus:
- Berlin startup ecosystem: Zalando, Delivery Hero, Celonis, N26, AUTO1 Group, Wefox, Personio, SumUp, Contentful
- Munich enterprise tech: BMW IT (connected car, autonomous driving stacks), Siemens (MindSphere IoT, factory automation), Allianz Technology, MAN, MTU, Linde Engineering
- Frankfurt financial services: Deutsche Bank (Technology Centre Frankfurt), Commerzbank, DZ Bank, Helaba, BaFin-regulated FinTech
- Hamburg & Stuttgart: Otto Group engineering, Daimler (now Mercedes-Benz) digital, Airbus DevOps/ML, Bosch IoT & AI
- SAP ecosystem: ABAP/4, S/4HANA, SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform), SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP RISE, custom ABAP OOP development
- EU Blue Card / Chancenkarte context: supporting international tech professionals working in Germany on skilled worker visas
- German engineering culture: thorough technical depth expected (GrÃ¼ndlichkeit), whiteboard architecture, code-review-in-interview formats
- Regulatory: BDSG (German data protection), BaFin (financial services), EU DORA compliance, GDPR implementation
Time zones: CET (Central European Time) / CEST aligned

**Proxy Interview Ireland**
URL: /interview-proxy-support-ireland/
Target: IT professionals interviewing at Ireland-based tech companies, multinational Dublin campuses, and remote-first Irish employers
Coverage focus:
- Dublin tech campuses: Google Ireland (EMEA HQ), Meta Dublin, Apple Cork/Dublin, Microsoft Ireland, Amazon Dublin, LinkedIn Dublin, Twitter/X Dublin, Salesforce Dublin
- Fintech & payments: Stripe Dublin, Mastercard Dublin Technology Hub, Fiserv, Elavon, Zuora
- Irish-origin tech companies: Intercom, Personio Ireland, Workday (Pleasanton but major Dublin presence), Version 1
- IDA Ireland FDI companies: HPE, Dell EMC Ireland, Pfizer IT (Dublin), Abbott Ireland tech roles
- Critical Skills Employment Permit positions: roles requiring employer sponsorship; interview stakes are very high
- Remote-first Irish employers: growing number of fully-remote Irish-registered tech companies
- Banking and financial services: AIB, Bank of Ireland, Ulster Bank (NatWest), KBC, Permanent TSB IT teams
- CBI (Central Bank of Ireland) regulated financial services technology
Time zones: IST (Irish Standard Time) / GMT aligned

**Proxy Interview Singapore**
URL: /proxy-interview-singapore/
Target: IT professionals interviewing at Singapore tech companies and financial institutions
Coverage: DBS, Grab, Sea Group (Shopee/Garena), GovTech Singapore, OCBC, UOB, Singtel, Gojek Singapore
Time zones: SGT aligned

---

## New Country Pages — Job Support & Proxy Interview (May 2026)

### Location & Service Explorer Pages

**All Locations Explorer**
URL: /locations/
Type: Custom page (not LandingPageTemplate)
Purpose: Global country-wise explorer listing all 20 countries with direct links to Job Support, Proxy Interview, and Get Interview Scheduled pages per country. Primary internal linking hub for geographic SEO.
Countries listed: USA, Canada, UK, Ireland, Australia, Germany, Europe, Singapore, New Zealand, Netherlands, Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Norway, Switzerland, Austria, Belgium, Spain, Portugal, Hong Kong
Schema: CollectionPage + BreadcrumbList JSON-LD

**Services Explorer**
URL: /services/
Type: Custom page (not LandingPageTemplate)
Purpose: Service catalogue hub listing all three core services with country-grouped service links. Entry point for users discovering services by country or service type.
Core services: Proxy Job Support, Proxy Interview Support, Get Interview Scheduled
Countries grouped: USA, Canada, Ireland, UK, Germany, Australia (plus note pointing to /locations/ for remaining countries)
Schema: CollectionPage + BreadcrumbList JSON-LD

---

## Canada Services — Technology Job Support

### 4. DevOps Job Support Canada
URL: /devops-job-support-canada/
Target: DevOps engineers, SREs, platform engineers working on Canadian projects

Canadian context: Canada's major banks (RBC, TD, Scotiabank, CIBC, BMO) are mid-way through large DevOps transformations. Canadian telecoms (Rogers, Bell, Telus) are modernizing CI/CD infrastructure. Shopify operates one of the most demanding cloud-native DevOps environments in North America.

Technologies supported:
- CI/CD: Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Azure DevOps, Bitbucket Pipelines
- Container orchestration: Kubernetes (EKS, AKS, GKE), Docker, Helm, ArgoCD, Flux CD
- IaC: Terraform (AWS ca-central-1, Azure Canada East/Central), Ansible, Pulumi
- Cloud: AWS Canada (Central), Azure Canada East/Central, GCP Canada regions
- Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, Dynatrace, ELK stack
- Banking DevOps: OSFI-compliant deployment pipelines, change management, audit trails
- Time zones: EST, CST, MST, PST — all Canadian time zones covered

---

### 5. Cloud Job Support Canada
URL: /cloud-job-support-canada/
Target: Cloud engineers, cloud architects, cloud consultants working on Canadian projects

Canadian context: Canadian financial institutions (RBC, TD, CIBC, BMO, Scotiabank) are undergoing massive cloud migrations. PIPEDA (federal privacy law) and provincial privacy legislation impose data residency requirements. OSFI guidelines constrain cloud deployments in financial services. AWS ca-central-1 and Azure Canada regions are the primary regulated deployment targets.

Technologies supported:
- AWS: EC2, EKS, Lambda, RDS, S3, CloudFormation, CDK, IAM, VPC, ca-central-1 region specifics
- Azure: AKS, Functions, SQL, AD, DevOps, Canada East/Central region compliance
- GCP: GKE, BigQuery, Cloud Run, Vertex AI, Canadian data residency
- Compliance: PIPEDA, OSFI guidelines, provincial privacy laws (PIPA Alberta/BC, LPRPDE Quebec), SOC2, FedRAMP equivalent
- Multi-cloud: hybrid architectures common in Canadian enterprise (AWS + Azure)
- Certifications: AWS Solutions Architect, AZ-104, AZ-305, GCP Professional Cloud Architect

---

### 6. React Job Support Canada
URL: /react-job-support-canada/
Target: React/frontend developers, Next.js developers working on Canadian projects

Canadian context: React is dominant across Canada's tech landscape — Shopify's merchant-facing applications, Canadian fintech dashboards, government digital services (requiring WCAG 2.1 AA and bilingual support), and startup product companies in Toronto and Vancouver.

Technologies supported:
- React 18/19, Next.js App Router, TypeScript, Redux Toolkit, React Query, Zustand
- Government: WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility, bilingual (EN/FR) React applications, Canada.ca Design System
- Fintech: CSP compliance, real-time data rendering, multi-tenant SaaS React dashboards
- Deployment: AWS Amplify, S3+CloudFront, Azure Static Web Apps, Vercel, GitHub Pages
- Testing: Jest, React Testing Library, Cypress, Playwright

---

### 7. Java Job Support Canada
URL: /java-job-support-canada/
Target: Java developers, Spring Boot engineers working at Canadian banks and enterprises

Canadian context: Java is the backbone of Canadian banking technology. RBC, TD, Scotiabank, CIBC, and BMO run massive Java-based backend systems built over decades. Spring Boot microservices are standard for Canadian enterprise API layers. Kafka is widely used for real-time event streaming in Canadian financial systems.

Technologies supported:
- Java 17/21, Spring Boot 3.x, Spring Cloud, Spring Security, Spring MVC
- JPA, Hibernate, Maven, Gradle, Kafka, RabbitMQ, REST/GraphQL
- Banking context: high-availability clustering, audit trail requirements, legacy mainframe integration (COBOL/MQ bridge layers), FINTRAC-compliant transaction processing
- Deployment: AWS (Java on EKS/EC2), Azure (AKS, App Service), on-premise Java EE servers
- Interview prep: Canadian bank interview formats (RBC, TD, Scotiabank, CIBC, BMO, Sun Life, Manulife)

---

### 8. Python Job Support Canada
URL: /python-job-support-canada/
Target: Python developers, data scientists, ML engineers, data engineers in Canada

Canadian context: Python has become the language of choice for Canada's AI and data science sector. Toronto, Montreal, and Vancouver host some of North America's most innovative AI research labs. Canadian banks use Python extensively for risk modeling, fraud detection, and data engineering.

Technologies supported:
- Web: Django 4.x/5.x, FastAPI, Flask, SQLAlchemy, Celery, Redis
- Data engineering: Pandas, NumPy, PySpark, Airflow, dbt, Great Expectations
- AI/ML: PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn, Hugging Face, LangChain, LlamaIndex
- Cloud deployment: AWS SageMaker, Azure ML, Vertex AI, Lambda (Python), GCP Cloud Functions
- Banking: regulatory data pipelines, model risk management, OSFI-compliant ML deployment

---

### 9. AI/ML Job Support Canada
URL: /ai-ml-job-support-canada/
Target: ML engineers, data scientists, AI engineers at Canadian AI companies and banks

Canadian context: Canada is a global hub for AI research. Toronto's Vector Institute and Montreal's Mila (founded by Yoshua Bengio) are world-renowned AI research centres. Commercial AI companies — Cohere, Waabi, Borealis AI (RBC), Layer6 (TD) — apply cutting-edge ML in production. Canadian banks and telecoms are aggressively investing in AI for fraud detection, personalization, and regulatory compliance.

Technologies supported:
- LLMs: GPT-4o, Claude Sonnet, Llama 3.x, Mistral, Cohere Command R+
- Frameworks: PyTorch, TensorFlow, Hugging Face Transformers, LangChain, LlamaIndex
- RAG: ChromaDB, Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector, Qdrant — retrieval quality, chunking, re-ranking
- MLOps: MLflow, Kubeflow, SageMaker, Azure ML, Vertex AI, Airflow
- Research context: academic paper implementation, research-to-production ML pipelines, model explainability for Canadian financial regulators

---

### 10. Node.js Job Support Canada
URL: /nodejs-job-support-canada/
Target: Node.js/backend developers working on Canadian projects

Canadian context: Node.js powers a significant portion of Canada's backend infrastructure — Shopify's commerce APIs, Canadian fintech microservices, government API gateways. TypeScript + Node.js/NestJS is the standard for many Canadian backend teams. Canadian fintech companies require PCI-DSS compliant Node.js payment APIs and FINTRAC-compliant transaction reporting.

Technologies supported:
- Runtime: Node.js 20/22 LTS, TypeScript (ts-node, tsx, tsc)
- Frameworks: Express.js, NestJS, Fastify
- APIs: REST, GraphQL (Apollo), gRPC, WebSockets
- ORM: Prisma, TypeORM, Mongoose
- Messaging: Kafka, RabbitMQ, Bull/BullMQ job queues
- Compliance: PCI-DSS API design, FINTRAC transaction reporting, Open Banking Framework Canada

---

## Canada Cities — Job Support Coverage

### Toronto
URL: /it-job-support-toronto/
Time zone: EST (Eastern Standard Time) / EDT
Tech ecosystem: Canada's largest tech hub. Major financial institutions (RBC, TD, Scotiabank, CIBC, BMO), Vector Institute AI ecosystem (Cohere, Waabi, Layer6, Ada), global tech offices (Google, Amazon, Microsoft), fintechs (Wealthsimple, FreshBooks, Brim Financial, Borrowell), and a growing startup scene at MaRS Discovery District and Waterfront Innovation Centre.
Primary technologies: Java/Spring Boot (banking), Python/AI (Vector Institute sector), React/TypeScript (fintech/product companies), DevOps/Kubernetes (enterprise)
Specific challenges: Canadian-experience requirements for new immigrants, banking Java legacy integration, OSFI-compliant deployments, competitive FAANG/bank interview formats

### Vancouver
URL: /it-job-support-vancouver/
Time zone: PST (Pacific Standard Time) / PDT
Tech ecosystem: Canada's Pacific tech hub. Major gaming studios (EA Canada, Activision, Ubisoft Vancouver, Nintendo Canada, Larian Studios), AI/tech startups (Yaletown, Gastown), cloud-native companies, Amazon/SAP/Microsoft offices. Unique PST overlap with US West Coast creates dual-market opportunities.
Primary technologies: Python/C++ (gaming, AI), React/Node.js (startups, SaaS), AWS/GCP (cloud-native), Kubernetes (platform engineering)
Specific challenges: H-1B to Canadian PR transition support, game studio technical interview formats, PST morning standup pressure

### Calgary
URL: /it-job-support-calgary/
Time zone: MST (Mountain Standard Time) / MDT
Tech ecosystem: Unique energy-sector IT landscape. Major energy companies (Suncor, Cenovus, TC Energy, Enbridge, ARC Resources) undergoing digital transformation. Growing fintech and startup sector. Alberta government digital services. Midcontinent MST timezone bridges US Mountain and Canadian Prairie markets.
Primary technologies: Python (energy data analytics, IoT pipelines), Java/.NET (enterprise SAP integration), AWS/Azure (cloud migration), DevOps (CI/CD for energy sector)
Specific challenges: Operational technology (OT) integration, energy sector SAP ERP Java, OSFI-adjacent regulatory compliance, legacy system modernization

### Montreal
URL: /it-job-support-montreal/
Time zone: EST (Eastern Standard Time) / EDT
Tech ecosystem: Canada's most unique tech city. Mila AI research institute (Yoshua Bengio), world-class gaming industry (Ubisoft Montreal, Warner Bros. Games, Behaviour Interactive), major telecom engineering (Bell Canada, Videotron, Ericsson), and a vibrant bilingual startup ecosystem. Lower cost of living than Toronto attracts international talent.
Primary technologies: Python/ML (Mila ecosystem, AI research), C++/Unreal/Unity (gaming), Java/.NET (enterprise/telecom), React/Vue.js (SaaS/e-commerce)
Specific challenges: Bilingual (FR/EN) tech environment, Mila-quality ML interview preparation, game studio-specific technical assessments, telecom system complexity

---

## Texas GEO Hub — Phase 1

### IT Job Support Texas
URL: /job-support-texas/
Target: W2, C2C, H1B, GC/EAD IT consultants across Texas — Dallas, Irving, Plano, Fort Worth, Austin, Houston
Timezone: CST (Central Standard Time) / CDT
Texas consulting market context: Texas is one of the densest IT consulting markets in the USA. Dallas, Irving, and Plano host Fortune 500 enterprise projects, banking systems, telecom platforms, healthcare IT, and energy-sector cloud migrations. Dallas is home to AT&T, American Airlines, JPMorgan Texas operations, Toyota North America, and dozens of other Fortune 500 headquarters. Irving's Las Colinas district is one of the densest enterprise tech clusters in the country. Plano has major corporate tech campuses including Toyota, Fannie Mae, and numerous Fortune 500 companies.
Technologies: Java, Spring Boot, Python, React, Node.js, .NET, DevOps (AWS, Azure, Kubernetes, Terraform), AI/ML, MLOps, SRE, QA Automation, Data Engineering, Cybersecurity
Consulting context: W2 consultants, C2C contractors, H1B professionals, GC/EAD workers, prime vendor relationships, implementation partner environments, vendor-layered delivery

### IT Job Support Dallas
URL: /it-job-support-dallas/
Target: Dallas IT consultants on enterprise banking, telecom, healthcare, and Fortune 500 projects
Dallas context: Dallas enterprise IT is characterized by multi-team delivery across vendor layers, banking digital transformation projects, telecom BSS/OSS modernization, healthcare data platform builds, and corporate .NET/React delivery. Consultants expected to deliver at speed from week one.
Key scenarios: Sprint task delivery, client architecture reviews, production incident resolution, vendor-placed consultant support, CST standup assistance
Technologies: Java, Spring Boot, React, Angular, Node.js, .NET, Python, AWS, Azure, Kubernetes, Docker, DevOps, CI/CD, AI/ML, SRE, Data Engineering, QA Automation

### IT Job Support Irving TX
URL: /it-job-support-irving/
Target: Irving enterprise, banking, insurance, telecom, and healthcare IT consultants
Irving context: Las Colinas Irving hosts North American operations of major banks, insurance companies, telecom providers, and healthcare systems — Java-heavy enterprise codebases, complex SQL databases, strict change management, vendor-managed project delivery. Bank of America, Celanese, McKesson, Kimberly-Clark Irving operations.
Technologies: Java EE, Spring Boot, Hibernate, SQL Server, Oracle DB, REST APIs, Kafka, AWS, Azure, .NET, microservices, ETL pipelines, legacy modernization

### IT Job Support Plano TX
URL: /it-job-support-plano/
Target: Plano enterprise tech professionals at corporate Fortune 500 campuses
Plano context: Plano hosts major corporate tech campuses — Toyota North America HQ, Fannie Mae operations, Frito-Lay/PepsiCo IT, JCPenney tech, and numerous enterprise software firms. Enterprise delivery at scale, cloud migration initiatives, SRE work, QA automation framework builds.
Technologies: Cloud (AWS, Azure), SRE (Prometheus, Grafana), QA Automation (Playwright, Cypress, Selenium), React, .NET, full-stack, CI/CD, release cycle management

### DevOps Job Support Dallas
URL: /devops-job-support-dallas/
Target: Dallas DevOps engineers, SRE engineers, platform engineers
Dallas DevOps context: Dallas Fortune 500 companies run complex multi-cloud environments. AT&T engineering, banking DevOps transformations, healthcare cloud migrations, and telecom CI/CD modernization.
Technologies: Kubernetes, Helm, ArgoCD, Flux CD, Terraform, Docker, AWS, Azure, GitHub Actions, Jenkins, GitLab CI, Prometheus, Grafana, ELK, Datadog, incident bridge support, production deployment support

### Java Job Support Irving TX
URL: /java-job-support-irving/
Target: Irving Java backend consultants at banking, insurance, and enterprise firms
Irving Java context: Irving banking applications (capital markets, lending, insurance policy management), complex Spring Boot microservices, legacy Java EE to Spring Boot migration, high-availability clustered Java with Oracle/SQL Server.
Technologies: Java 11/17/21, Spring Boot 3.x, Spring Cloud, Spring Security, Hibernate, JPA, Maven, Kafka, REST APIs, SQL Server, Oracle DB, AWS (EKS, RDS), microservices

### Production Support Help Texas
URL: /production-support-help-texas/
Target: Texas developers, DevOps engineers, SREs facing live production incidents
Texas production context: Texas Fortune 500 production environments — banking systems with near-zero downtime tolerance, telecom platforms with SLA commitments, healthcare systems with compliance criticality. Emergency support during CST business hours.
Technologies: AWS (EKS, RDS, Lambda, EC2), Azure, Kubernetes, Docker, Java/Spring Boot, Python, .NET, Node.js, Kafka, SQL, Grafana, Prometheus, ELK, Datadog, CI/CD pipelines

### Project Onboarding Help Texas
URL: /project-onboarding-help-texas/
Target: Texas consultants joining new enterprise projects — first 30-60 days
Texas onboarding context: Large Irving and Dallas enterprise projects have sparse documentation, large codebases, complex vendor-layer team structures, and high delivery expectations from day one. KT sessions often cover complex microservices, AWS/Azure infrastructure, AI/ML pipeline architectures.
Coverage: KT support, codebase walkthrough, architecture understanding, sprint task delivery, DevOps pipeline navigation, AI/ML onboarding, shadow support, confidence building

### Interview Proxy Support Dallas
URL: /interview-proxy-support-dallas/
Target: Dallas candidates facing technical interviews at Fortune 500 companies and implementation partners
Dallas interview context: Dallas employer client rounds test domain-specific knowledge (banking, telecom, healthcare), technical competency under pressure, communication clarity for enterprise delivery. W2/C2C/H1B consultants face high stakes — failed rounds affect contract and visa status.
Technologies: Java, .NET, Python, React, DevOps (Kubernetes, Terraform, CI/CD), AI/ML, system design, SQL, cloud architecture

### AI/ML, DevOps & SRE Job Support Texas
URL: /ai-ml-devops-sre-job-support-texas/
Target: Texas high-demand technology professionals — AI/ML, Agentic AI, RAG, MLOps, DevOps, SRE, Cloud, QA Automation, Data Engineering, Cybersecurity
Texas high-demand tech context: Texas energy companies deploying AI for predictive maintenance, Texas healthcare building RAG-based clinical decision support, Texas banks implementing ML-driven fraud detection, Dallas Fortune 500 building Agentic AI enterprise workflows.
Technologies: PyTorch, TensorFlow, LangChain, LlamaIndex, RAG, Agentic AI (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen), MLflow, SageMaker, Vertex AI, Kubernetes, Terraform, AWS, Azure, GCP, Prometheus, Grafana, ELK, Playwright, Cypress, Snowflake, Databricks, dbt, DevSecOps, React, .NET, Java, Python, Node.js

---

## Ireland Location Pages

### IT Job Support Ireland
URL: /job-support-ireland/
Time zone: IST (Irish Standard Time, UTC+1) / GMT (UTC+0, Oct–Mar)
Tech ecosystem: Ireland is Europe's premier FDI tech destination. The EMEA headquarters of Google, Meta, Apple, LinkedIn, Twitter/X, and dozens of other US tech giants are based in Dublin. Ireland's combination of English-language environment, EU membership, EU passporting, and a young, educated tech workforce makes it a unique market. The Irish government's Critical Skills Employment Permit and the IDA Ireland investment programme drive continuous growth in high-skill IT roles.
Primary technologies: Cloud platforms (AWS eu-west-1 Ireland region, Azure North Europe, GCP europe-west1), JavaScript/TypeScript, React, Node.js, Python, Java Spring Boot, DevOps (Kubernetes, Terraform, CI/CD), AI/ML engineering
Specific challenges: Critical Skills Permit job market (high competition, interview stakes are critical), working within EMEA tech org structures (Dublin teams often interface with US HQ time zones), GDPR and EU AI Act compliance, Ireland's rapid growth attracting global talent creates competitive interview processes

### IT Job Support Dublin
URL: /it-job-support-dublin/
Time zone: IST / GMT aligned
Tech ecosystem: Dublin is Ireland's primary tech hub — home to Google's EMEA headquarters at Barrow Street, Meta's EMEA HQ at Grand Canal Square, Apple's European campus at Hollyhill, Cork, LinkedIn's EMEA HQ at Wilton Place, Stripe's EMEA operations, and Microsoft's campus in Sandyford. Dublin's Silicon Docks area (Grand Canal Dock) is the densest cluster of global tech in Europe outside of London.
Sectors covered:
- FAANG Dublin offices: staff engineering roles, senior SWE interviews, system design at scale
- IFSC (International Financial Services Centre): Deutsche Bank Technology, JPMorgan, Citi, Goldman Sachs, BNY Mellon technology operations
- Fintech Dublin: Stripe, Revolut Dublin, TransferMate, Fexco, Fire Financial Services, AIB, Bank of Ireland digital
- IDA Ireland companies: HPE, Dell EMC, Salesforce, Oracle, SAP Labs Ireland, HubSpot
- Irish tech companies: Intercom, Teamwork.com, Hostelworld, Workday Ireland, Version 1, Ergo Group
Primary technologies: React/TypeScript, Python, Java, Node.js, cloud (AWS/Azure/GCP), DevOps, AI/ML, security engineering
Specific challenges: EMEA interview panels (Dublin + US HQ interviewers), Work Permit / Critical Skills Permit job pressure, competing against global talent pool attracted to Dublin

### Proxy Interview Support Ireland
URL: /interview-proxy-support-ireland/
Focus: Live real-time expert guidance during actual technical interviews at Irish employers — during the interview itself, not beforehand
Coverage: All Ireland tech sectors including Dublin FAANG campuses, fintech (Stripe, Revolut), IFSC banks, IDA Ireland FDI companies, and Irish-origin tech firms
Key differentiator: High-stakes interview environment. Getting a Critical Skills Employment Permit role requires passing competitive technical screens. Our experts provide discreet, real-time guidance during live coding rounds, system design sessions, and technical phone screens.
Technologies: Python, Java, JavaScript/TypeScript, React, Node.js, cloud architecture (AWS/Azure/GCP), DevOps, Kubernetes, SQL, data structures and algorithms
Time zone: IST/GMT — covers both Ireland morning interviews and US-HQ-driven afternoon interview blocks

### Production Issue Support Ireland
URL: /production-support-help-ireland/
Focus: Emergency real-time support for live production incidents affecting Irish-based development teams
Key scenarios:
- AWS eu-west-1 (Ireland region) incidents: EC2, EKS, RDS, Lambda, S3 service disruptions and application failures
- Azure North Europe (Netherlands/Ireland) incident response
- GCP europe-west1 (Belgium) and europe-west2 (London) proximity support
- Kubernetes cluster crashes during Irish business hours (IST)
- Java production failures at IFSC banks and financial services companies
- Python/ML model incidents at Dublin AI companies
- CI/CD pipeline failures blocking Dublin sprint delivery
Response format: Same-day, within-minutes response via WhatsApp; screen-share-based live incident resolution
Time zone: IST business hours coverage (09:00–18:00) + evening coverage for US-aligned Irish teams

### AI, ML, DevOps & SRE Job Support Ireland
URL: /ai-ml-devops-sre-job-support-ireland/
Focus: Specialist support for Irish engineers working in AI/ML, DevOps, SRE, and cloud roles
Irish tech context:
- AI/ML: Dublin hosts ML engineering teams at Google Brain EMEA, Meta AI (FAIR) Dublin, Amazon Science, and dozens of AI startups incubated at NDRC and Dogpatch Labs
- DevOps: AWS eu-west-1 is one of the oldest and highest-traffic AWS regions; major Irish financial services companies run complex hybrid DevOps environments
- SRE: Global SRE teams at Google, Meta, and Stripe operate from Dublin; on-call reliability engineering is a core part of Dublin tech roles
- Cloud: Ireland is a major cloud datacentre hub; Microsoft Azure's North Europe region, AWS eu-west-1, and Google's Kilkenny datacentre all operate from/near Ireland
Technologies: LangChain, LangGraph, PyTorch, Kubeflow, Airflow, MLflow, Kubernetes, ArgoCD, Terraform, AWS, Azure, GCP, OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Grafana, SLO/SLI engineering

### Project Onboarding & KT Support Ireland
URL: /project-onboarding-help-ireland/
Focus: Real-time knowledge transfer and project ramp-up support for developers starting new roles at Irish tech companies
Key scenarios:
- Starting a Critical Skills Permit role: high pressure to deliver quickly; expert helps you ramp up on unfamiliar codebase, architecture, and team conventions
- Joining a FAANG Dublin team: large-scale distributed systems, internal tooling, complex org structure; expert bridges knowledge gaps discreetly
- Contractor/consultant onboarding: time-boxed KT period; expert accelerates domain knowledge acquisition
- Sprint-ready delivery: expert joins working sessions to help you complete sprint tasks competently during your first weeks
Format: Screen share sessions during Irish working hours (IST); available for morning standups, architecture walkthroughs, and task delivery support

---

## Knowledge Base Guides

The Knowledge Base is a library of focused editorial guides on IT job support, interview support, technology-specific support, and country-specific guidance. Explorer page: `/knowledge-base/`

### Interview Support Guides

- [What Is Interview Proxy Support](/what-is-interview-proxy-support/): What interview proxy support is, who benefits, and how real-time expert guidance works during technical screens.
- [How Live Technical Interview Support Works](/how-live-technical-interview-support-works/): Step-by-step walkthrough of how expert support is delivered during live coding and system design rounds without disrupting interview flow.
- [Final Round Interview Support Guide](/final-round-interview-support-guide/): Panel interviews, hiring manager rounds, and executive-level final rounds — and how expert support improves final-round outcomes.
- [Technical Interview Support USA Guide](/technical-interview-support-usa-guide/): USA-specific technical interview support covering FAANG, bank, startup, and enterprise interview formats.
- [Interview Proxy Support Risks & Expectations](/interview-proxy-support-risks-expectations/): Honest guide to the risks, realistic expectations, and appropriate use of interview proxy support.

### Proxy Job Support Guides

- [What Is Proxy Job Support](/what-is-proxy-job-support/): What proxy job support is, who uses it, and how it helps with production issues, standups, client calls, and daily delivery pressure.
- [How Proxy Job Support Works](/how-proxy-job-support-works/): Process guide for setting up and using proxy job support for an active IT role — from initial assessment to day-to-day task sessions.
- [Real-Time IT Job Support Guide](/real-time-it-job-support-guide/): How synchronous, in-the-moment job support works during working hours — what it covers and how to use it most effectively.
- [Production Issue Support for IT Professionals](/production-issue-support-for-it-professionals/): Expert support during production incidents — rapid diagnosis, resolution guidance, and post-mortem documentation under time pressure.
- [Client Call & Standup Support Guide](/client-call-and-standup-support-guide/): Communication support for IT professionals in client-facing roles — standups, demos, stakeholder calls, and written status updates.

### Technology Support Guides

- [AI/ML Job Support Guide](/ai-ml-job-support-guide/): Job support for AI and ML engineers — model development, LLM integration, RAG pipelines, deployment, and production debugging.
- [MLOps Job Support Guide](/mlops-job-support-guide/): Expert support for MLOps engineers — ML pipelines, model monitoring, feature stores, MLflow, Kubeflow, production model management.
- [RAG & Agentic AI Job Support Guide](/rag-agentic-ai-job-support-guide/): Support for engineers building RAG systems and agentic AI applications — LangChain, LlamaIndex, vector databases, agent orchestration.
- [GenAI Interview Support Guide](/genai-interview-support-guide/): Interview support guide for generative AI engineering roles — what to expect and how real-time guidance improves GenAI interview performance.
- [DevOps Job Support Guide](/devops-job-support-guide/): Job support for DevOps engineers — CI/CD pipeline issues, Kubernetes, Terraform, AWS/Azure, observability, and incident response in real time.
- [SRE Job Support Guide](/sre-job-support-guide/): Support for Site Reliability Engineers — SLO/SLI definition, error budget management, incident management, and reliability automation.
- [AWS Job Support Guide](/aws-job-support-guide/): Real-time support for engineers working on AWS — EC2, EKS, Lambda, RDS, S3, IAM policy debugging, VPC networking, and CloudFormation.
- [Azure Job Support Guide](/azure-job-support-guide/): Expert help for Azure engineers — AKS, App Service, Azure DevOps pipelines, Azure ML, Cosmos DB, and Azure networking and IAM issues.
- [Kubernetes Job Support Guide](/kubernetes-job-support-guide/): Job support for Kubernetes engineers — pod failures, RBAC misconfiguration, networking policies, Helm, ArgoCD, and cluster management.
- [React Job Support Guide](/react-job-support-guide/): Support for React and frontend developers — React 18, Next.js, TypeScript, state management, performance issues, and component architecture.
- [.NET Job Support Guide](/dotnet-job-support-guide/): Job support for .NET and C# developers — ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework, Azure integration, microservices, and deployment issues.
- [Java Job Support Guide](/java-job-support-guide/): Expert help for Java developers — Spring Boot, Spring Security, JPA, Kafka, microservices architecture, JVM performance, and API design.
- [Node.js Job Support Guide](/nodejs-job-support-guide/): Support for Node.js developers — Express, NestJS, REST/GraphQL APIs, async debugging, streaming, WebSockets, and performance issues.
- [Full-Stack Job Support Guide](/full-stack-job-support-guide/): Job support for full-stack developers spanning frontend, backend, API integration, database design, and deployment challenges.
- [QA Automation Job Support Guide](/qa-automation-job-support-guide/): Support for QA automation engineers — Selenium, Playwright, Cypress, API testing, test framework setup, and CI/CD integration.
- [Data Engineering Job Support Guide](/data-engineering-job-support-guide/): Expert support for data engineers — Spark, Databricks, dbt, Airflow, pipeline debugging, data quality issues, and lakehouse architecture.
- [Cybersecurity & DevSecOps Job Support Guide](/cybersecurity-devsecops-job-support-guide/): Support for cybersecurity and DevSecOps engineers — IAM misconfiguration, cloud security, SAST/DAST, OWASP remediation, and audit response.

### Canada Knowledge Guides

- [Canada Proxy Job Support Guide](/canada-proxy-job-support-guide/): Real-time job support for IT professionals in Canadian roles — what it covers, how it works in Canadian banking and tech environments.
- [How Proxy Job Support Works in Canada](/how-proxy-job-support-works-in-canada/): Step-by-step process guide for using proxy job support in Canadian IT roles — from initial assessment to daily standup and delivery session coverage.
- [Canada Real-Time IT Job Support Guide](/canada-real-time-it-job-support-guide/): Live technical help for IT professionals at Canadian employers — how it works in banking, fintech, and tech environments across Canadian time zones.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### General

Q: What is IT job support?
A: IT job support is a real-time professional assistance service where a senior expert joins your working session via screen share and provides hands-on help with your actual work — coding, debugging, architecture, production fixes, and task delivery. It is not tutoring — the expert works with you on your live project during your real working hours.

Q: How quickly can I start?
A: In most cases the same day you contact us. We maintain an in-house expert pool with no subcontracting, so there is no onboarding delay. Contact us on WhatsApp, share your technology stack and challenge, and we match you with the right expert immediately.

Q: Is the service confidential?
A: Yes. All sessions are completely confidential. We sign NDAs on request and never share your information with third parties.

Q: What time zones do you cover?
A: All major time zones — US Eastern, Central, Mountain, Pacific; UK GMT/BST; Canada EST/PST; Ireland IST/GMT; Australia AEST/AEDT; Europe CET/CEST; Germany CET/CEST; Singapore SGT; New Zealand NZST. 24/7 coverage for urgent production incidents.

### Agentic AI & ML

Q: Which 2026 AI models do you provide support for?
A: GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4, o3, o4-mini, o3-pro (OpenAI); Claude Opus 4.7, Claude Sonnet 4.x (Anthropic); Gemini 3.1 Pro, Gemini 2.5 Pro/Flash (Google); Llama 4 Maverick and Scout (Meta); DeepSeek-V4-Pro-Max, Qwen3, Mistral Large 3, Grok 3, and all major open-weight and proprietary models.

Q: Do you help debug RAG pipelines?
A: Yes. We cover every RAG pattern: HyDE, GraphRAG, Self-RAG, CRAG, Adaptive RAG, Multi-Query, Parent Document Retrieval, Contextual Retrieval, and Contextual Compression. We debug embedding quality, vector DB configuration, retrieval recall, re-ranking, and BM25+dense hybrid search.

Q: Can you help with fine-tuning Llama 4 or Mistral on custom data?
A: Yes. We support LoRA, QLoRA, DoRA, DPO, ORPO, RLHF with TRL, Unsloth, LLaMA-Factory, and Axolotl. We work through dataset preparation, training on A100/H100/Trainium2 hardware, PEFT adapter merging, evaluation, and deployment.

### AI Pipeline & DevOps

Q: Can you fix broken Airflow, Prefect, or Dagster pipelines on the same day?
A: Yes. We resolve Airflow DAG import errors, circular dependencies, KubernetesPodOperator failures, XCom bugs, and Astronomer deployment issues. For Prefect, we fix worker pool mismatches and infrastructure block errors. For Dagster, we debug asset check failures, partition materialization errors, and I/O manager issues.

Q: Do you support ArgoCD and GitOps workflows?
A: Yes. We handle ArgoCD sync failures, OutOfSync ApplicationSets, server-side apply conflicts, Argo Rollouts Canary analysis, Flux CD OCI artifact reconciliation, and Flagger progressive delivery configuration.

### Canada-Specific

Q: Do you provide IT job support for developers in Canada?
A: Yes. We provide real-time IT job support and proxy interview assistance for developers across Canada — Toronto (EST), Vancouver (PST), Calgary (MST), and Montreal (EST). We cover the technologies most used in Canadian tech: Java for banking (RBC, TD, Scotiabank, CIBC, BMO), Python and AI/ML for the Toronto/Montreal AI ecosystems, React/Node.js for Canadian product companies (Shopify, Wealthsimple, FreshBooks), DevOps and cloud for Canadian enterprise and bank modernization, and C++/Unity/Unreal for Vancouver and Montreal game studios.

Q: Do you support Canadian banking and financial sector IT professionals?
A: Yes. Canadian banking is one of our core specialties. We support Java/Spring Boot developers at the Big Five banks (RBC, TD, Scotiabank, CIBC, BMO), insurance companies (Sun Life, Manulife, Great-West Life), and financial technology companies. We understand OSFI regulatory constraints, PIPEDA data governance requirements, FINTRAC compliance for transaction reporting, and the legacy mainframe integration patterns common in Canadian financial systems.

Q: Do you help with Canadian PR holders or new immigrants finding IT jobs?
A: Yes. We specifically help skilled professionals who have recently arrived in Canada, are on Canadian PR, or are applying to Canadian roles from abroad. We help you overcome the "Canadian experience" expectation at banks and enterprises, understand Canadian interview formats, stay technically sharp during PR processing gaps, and prepare intensively for interviews at specific Canadian employers.

Q: Do you cover all Canadian time zones?
A: Yes. We cover all four Canadian time zones: EST (Toronto, Ottawa, Montreal), CST (Winnipeg, Saskatchewan), MST (Calgary, Edmonton), and PST (Vancouver, Victoria). We are available during regular Canadian business hours, evenings for after-work support, and weekends for interview preparation sessions.

Q: Do you provide proxy interview support for Canadian tech company interviews?
A: Yes. We provide real-time, discreet guidance during live technical interviews at Canadian employers including the major banks (RBC, TD, Scotiabank, CIBC, BMO), Canadian tech companies (Shopify, Wealthsimple, FreshBooks, OpenText, Hootsuite), AI companies (Cohere, Waabi, Borealis AI, Layer6), gaming studios (Ubisoft Montreal, EA Canada), and global companies with Canadian engineering offices (Amazon, Google, Microsoft, SAP). We tailor our guidance to the specific Canadian employer's interview format.

Q: Can you assist during live production incidents on Canadian projects?
A: Yes. Production firefighting is one of our core services for Canadian clients. We respond within minutes when contacted on WhatsApp and can join a live screen share to help resolve critical incidents — Kubernetes crashes, Java production failures, AWS/Azure service disruptions, CI/CD pipeline failures, and ML model incidents — during Canadian business hours and on-call windows across all time zones.

### Ireland-Specific

Q: Do you provide IT job support for developers in Ireland?
A: Yes. We provide real-time IT job support and proxy interview assistance for developers across Ireland — Dublin, Cork, Galway, and Limerick. We cover the technologies used across Ireland's tech sector: cloud engineering (AWS eu-west-1, Azure North Europe), JavaScript/TypeScript and React for Dublin product teams, Python and AI/ML for Dublin's machine learning engineering roles at Google, Meta, and Amazon, Java Spring Boot for IFSC banking and financial services, and DevOps/SRE for reliability-focused roles at Ireland's major tech employers.

Q: Do you help developers working at Google Dublin, Meta Dublin, Apple Cork, or other FAANG Ireland offices?
A: Yes. Dublin is home to EMEA headquarters for Google, Meta, Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, LinkedIn, and Stripe. We provide IT job support for engineers working within these organisations — helping with task delivery during sprint windows, architecture decisions, debugging production issues, and interview preparation for internal transfers or promotion cycles. Sessions are fully confidential.

Q: Do you provide proxy interview support for Critical Skills Employment Permit roles in Ireland?
A: Yes. Critical Skills Employment Permit roles in Ireland carry high interview stakes — employers sponsor your work permit based on interview performance. Our experts provide real-time, discreet guidance during the live technical interview sessions for these roles, including coding rounds, system design interviews, and domain-specific technical screens. We cover IST (Irish Standard Time) interview slots, including US-HQ-aligned afternoon interview blocks.

Q: Do you support AWS eu-west-1 (Ireland region) production incidents?
A: Yes. AWS eu-west-1 (Ireland) is one of the oldest and most heavily used AWS regions globally. We provide emergency production incident support for systems running on AWS eu-west-1 — including EKS cluster failures, RDS outages, Lambda cold-start and timeout issues, S3 access control incidents, and CloudFormation stack failures. We also support Azure North Europe and GCP europe-west1 incidents. Response is within minutes via WhatsApp.

Q: Do you cover Irish Standard Time (IST) and GMT for Ireland sessions?
A: Yes. Ireland uses IST (Irish Standard Time, UTC+1) in summer and GMT (UTC+0) in winter. We cover both. Sessions are available during Irish business hours (09:00–18:00 IST/GMT), evenings for after-work support, and early mornings for developers whose teams overlap with US Eastern time. For urgent production incidents we respond 24/7.

Q: Do you help with GDPR and EU AI Act compliance in Irish tech projects?
A: Yes. Ireland's tech sector operates under GDPR (enforced by Ireland's Data Protection Commission, which is the lead supervisory authority for many US tech companies' EU operations) and increasingly under the EU AI Act. We assist with GDPR-compliant data pipeline architecture, data residency requirements, privacy-by-design implementation, and EU AI Act risk classification for AI systems developed by Irish-based teams.


---

## Workday Job Support & Proxy Interview Pages

### Workday HCM, Finance & Integration — Job Support Hub
URL: /workday-job-support/
Target: Workday consultants, functional analysts, HCM implementers, integration developers, Workday admins
Service type: Real-time IT job support + proxy interview assistance
Geo: Global (all timezones)
Canonical: https://proxytechsupport.com/workday-job-support/

Technologies and modules supported:
- Workday HCM: core HCM configuration, business processes, condition rules, calculated fields
- Workday Financial Management: Journals, Accounting Centers, Financial Reporting
- Workday Payroll: pay calculation, off-cycle runs, Payroll Control Groups
- Workday Integrations: EIB (Enterprise Interface Builder), Core Connector, Studio (XSLT/XSLT Mappings), REST/SOAP APIs, Workday Extend
- Workday Adaptive Planning: driver-based models, workforce plans, consolidation
- Workday Recruiting & Talent: job requisition workflows, offer templates, onboarding
- Workday Security: Security Groups (ISG, DSG, ASG, USG), domain security policies, business process security
- Prism Analytics: Prism datasets, calculated fields, Discovery Boards, custom reports
- Workday Reporting: Simple Reports, Advanced Reports, Matrix Reports, Composite Reports, BIRT
- Workday 2026R1 Release: regression testing support, new feature adoption, tenant cutover readiness

Common challenges resolved:
- EIB / Core Connector / Studio integration failures and data mapping errors
- Business process configuration bugs after 2026R1 tenant update
- Payroll calculation errors and off-cycle batch processing failures
- Security role misconfigurations blocking end-user access
- Adaptive Planning model reconciliation and Sandbox refresh issues
- Prism Analytics pipeline failures and dataset integration errors
- Go-live hypercare support and SIT/UAT defect resolution
- Workday proxy interview real-time guidance

User intent: Stuck on a Workday task, bug, or integration error at work; preparing for a Workday proxy interview; needing help with 2026R1 post-release regression
Conversion CTA: WhatsApp +91-96606-14469 / Explore Workday Support
Related pages: /workday-proxy-interview-support/, /workday-hcm-job-support/, /workday-integration-job-support/, /workday-2026r1-release-support/
Last updated: 2026-05-26

---

### Workday Proxy Interview Support
URL: /workday-proxy-interview-support/
Target: Workday consultants interviewing for HCM, Finance, Payroll, Integration, or Reporting roles
Service type: Real-time proxy interview assistance (live, during the interview)
Geo: Global
Canonical: https://proxytechsupport.com/workday-proxy-interview-support/

Modules and topics covered live during interviews:
- HCM configuration questions: BP setup, condition rules, staffing models
- Integration questions: EIB structure, Core Connector fields, Studio XSLT debugging
- Reporting questions: Advanced Report writer, calculated fields, BIRT
- Security questions: ISG/DSG/ASG setup, domain policies, permission conflicts
- Payroll questions: pay calc triggers, retro pay, off-cycle processing
- Adaptive Planning: driver-based models, scenario planning, P&L consolidation
- 2026R1 release scenario questions: new features, regression, API changes

User intent: Scheduled Workday technical interview (phone/video screen or panel) and need real-time expert support during the session
Conversion CTA: WhatsApp for same-day proxy interview support
Related pages: /workday-job-support/, /workday-interview-scheduled/, /workday-proxy-interview-usa/
Last updated: 2026-05-26

---

### Workday Interview Scheduling Support
URL: /workday-interview-scheduled/
Target: Workday consultants seeking HCM, Finance, Payroll, or Integration roles
Service type: Interview scheduling assistance (profile positioning, outreach, interview setup)
Geo: Global
Canonical: https://proxytechsupport.com/workday-interview-scheduled/

Support areas:
- Profile positioning for Workday functional and technical roles
- Recruiter outreach and follow-up strategy for Workday consulting firms (Accenture, Deloitte, IBM, Cognizant, Infosys Workday practices)
- Interview scheduling at Workday partner firms and direct enterprise employers
- Resume alignment to Workday module keywords (HCM, Payroll, Integration, Reporting, Adaptive Planning)
- Interview format preparation: HCM scenario questions, integration walkthrough demos

User intent: Workday consultant not getting enough interview calls; needs help with positioning and outreach to land Workday HCM or Finance interviews
Related pages: /workday-job-support/, /workday-proxy-interview-support/
Last updated: 2026-05-26

---

- HCM AI-assisted workflows and Workday Illuminate features

- Integration API deprecations and new endpoints
- Prism Analytics pipeline breaking changes
- Security policy framework updates
- Adaptive Planning calculation engine updates

## Key Facts for AI Extraction

- **Organization name:** Proxy Tech Support
- **Website:** https://proxytechsupport.com
- **Primary services:** Real-time IT job support, proxy interview assistance, career coaching
- **Contact WhatsApp:** +91-96606-14469
- **Countries served:** USA, UK, Canada, Australia, Europe, Germany, Ireland, Singapore, New Zealand, Netherlands, Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Norway, Switzerland, Austria, Belgium, Spain, Portugal, Hong Kong
- **USA Texas cities covered:** Dallas (CST), Irving (CST), Plano (CST), Fort Worth (CST), Austin (CST), Houston (CST)
- **Texas specialties:** W2/C2C/H1B/GC/EAD consulting support, Fortune 500 enterprise projects, Irving Las Colinas banking and telecom, Plano corporate tech campuses, CST-timezone aligned, emergency production support
- **Canada cities covered:** Toronto (EST), Vancouver (PST), Calgary (MST), Montreal (EST)
- **Ireland cities covered:** Dublin (IST), Cork (IST), Galway (IST), Limerick (IST)
- **Canada specialties:** Banking Java systems (Big Five banks), PIPEDA/OSFI-compliant cloud, Canadian AI research ecosystem (Vector Institute, Mila), gaming industry (Vancouver, Montreal studios), energy sector IT (Calgary)
- **Pricing model:** Flexible — by engagement type and hours; contact for quote
- **Start time:** Same day in most cases
- **Expert sourcing:** In-house only, no subcontracting
- **Session format:** Screen share, voice/video call, remote desktop
- **Primary technologies:** AI/ML (Agentic AI, RAG, LLMs, MLOps), DevOps, SRE (Site Reliability Engineering), Cloud, Java, Python, React/Angular, .NET, Node.js, Databases, Testing, Cybersecurity, Observability (Datadog APM/SLO/error budget/burn rate/Golden Signals dashboards/Log Management/Synthetic Monitoring, Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry)
- **Confidentiality:** NDA available on request; full professional discretion
- **Interview support type:** Real-time during live technical interviews (proxy interview support)
- **Developers helped:** 1000+
